project: Adult Player Lifecycle — Phase 8: WhatsApp Wellness Check Integration
description: Delivers daily wellness check-ins via WhatsApp using the existing Twilio integration. Players receive a configurable-time daily message with each wellness dimension as a numbered question (1–5). Answers are collected via a conversational sequential flow managed by a new whatsappWellnessSessions state table. On completion, responses are submitted directly to dailyPlayerHealthChecks (same table as app submissions). Extends the existing processIncomingMessage action — wellness session routing takes priority over coach routing when an active session is detected. Includes player opt-in, admin scheduling config, duplicate handling (skip if already submitted via app), and completion/summary reply.
readinessLevel: 100% - Twilio WhatsApp infrastructure is fully live. Webhook at /whatsapp/incoming routes to internal.actions.whatsapp.processIncomingMessage. whatsappMessages table, whatsappSessions table, phoneUtils, duplicateDetection, and command handler all exist. Phase 4 (dailyPlayerHealthChecks schema + submitDailyHealthCheck mutation) must be complete first.

criticalContext: Phase 4 MUST be complete first — this phase writes to dailyPlayerHealthChecks using the same submitDailyHealthCheck mutation as the app.

EXTEND EXISTING: Do NOT create a new Twilio client or webhook. The webhook at /whatsapp/incoming already routes to internal.actions.whatsapp.processIncomingMessage. Extend that action to detect and handle wellness sessions FIRST before falling through to the coach voice note flow.

EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE TO REUSE: normalizePhoneNumber() utility (packages/backend/convex/lib/phoneUtils.ts), whatsappMessages table (for logging inbound), whatsappPendingMessages table (for outbound queue), duplicateDetection utilities, TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID / TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN / TWILIO_WHATSAPP_FROM env vars already set.

SESSION PRIORITY RULE: In processIncomingMessage, the FIRST check must be: does this phone number have an active whatsappWellnessSession today? If YES → route to wellness handler and RETURN. Do NOT fall through to coach processing. This prevents coach voice note processing of wellness replies.

Coaches who are also players: their phone number may be used for both coach voice notes AND player wellness. Session priority handles this — if a wellness session is active, their reply is wellness. If no active wellness session, their message is processed as a coach note.

OUTBOUND MESSAGES: Use the existing Twilio REST API pattern in the codebase. Messages are text-based (not interactive list messages — Twilio Sandbox and most production WhatsApp via Twilio does not support interactive list messages). Use numbered emoji options in the message body: 1️⃣ 2️⃣ 3️⃣ 4️⃣ 5️⃣

TEMPLATE MESSAGE REQUIREMENT: The initial outbound daily message MUST use a Twilio/Meta pre-approved message template if it is the first contact with the player or if >24 hours have passed since their last message. In Sandbox mode this is waived. For production, a template must be registered with Meta — the PRD includes the template content.

PLAYER OPT-IN IS MANDATORY per WhatsApp Business Policy and GDPR. Players must explicitly opt in via the app before any messages are sent. WhatsApp number must be stored in E.164 format. Players can opt out by replying STOP (handled by Twilio) or by toggling off in app settings.
SESSION EXPIRY: Wellness sessions expire 8 hours after the outbound message is sent. If player replies after expiry, respond: 'This wellness check has expired. Your next check will arrive tomorrow.'

DAILY DEDUP: Before sending the daily outbound message, check dailyPlayerHealthChecks using by_player_and_date index. If a record exists for today (submitted via app or a previous WhatsApp session), send a 'already checked in' message instead of starting a new session.
Always use .withIndex() — NEVER .filter() in Convex. All new queries/mutations must include returns validator.
BetterAuth tables use ctx.runQuery(components.betterAuth.adapter.*). Application tables use ctx.db.
keyFiles: packages/backend/convex/actions/whatsapp.ts — READ FULLY. Extend processIncomingMessage to check for wellness sessions FIRST. Find the sendWhatsAppMessage / Twilio REST call pattern to reuse for outbound.
packages/backend/convex/http.ts — do NOT modify. Webhook already routes correctly.

packages/backend/convex/schema.ts — ADD whatsappWellnessSessions table. ADD whatsappOptIn/whatsappNumber fields to playerWellnessSettings (or create separate playerContactPreferences table — check schema to decide).
packages/backend/convex/models/playerHealthChecks.ts — submitDailyHealthCheck mutation (Phase 4). Wellness WhatsApp flow calls this on completion.
packages/backend/convex/models/playerIdentities.ts — find how to look up a playerIdentity by phone number (or add index if needed)
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/player/settings/page.tsx — Phase 4 settings page. Add WhatsApp opt-in section.
apps/web/src/app/orgs/[orgId]/admin/ — find wellness reminder settings from Phase 4. Extend with WhatsApp scheduling config.

criticalPatterns.SessionFirstRouting: In processIncomingMessage: step 1 = normalizePhoneNumber. Step 2 = query whatsappWellnessSessions for active session today. Step 3 = if active session found → call handleWellnessReply() and return. Step 4 = existing coach processing continues unchanged.
criticalPatterns.PhoneLookupForPlayers: To find the player from an inbound WhatsApp message: query playerWellnessSettings (or playerContactPreferences) by the normalised phone number. If no match found AND no active session: fall through to coach processing.
criticalPatterns.OutboundTextFormat:
Use plain text messages with emoji numbers. Example: '1️⃣ Very Poor
2️⃣ Poor
3️⃣ Neutral
4️⃣ Good
5️⃣ Great'. Do NOT attempt interactive list messages — Twilio production accounts do not universally support them.
criticalPatterns.TemplateForFirstMessage: The initial daily outbound must use a Meta-approved template content-id via Twilio. In sandbox: can use free text. For production: register the wellness template (see US-P8-001 for template content). Any reply from the player within 24 hours makes subsequent messages free-form.
criticalPatterns.InputValidation: When processing a wellness reply, accept: '1', '2', '3', '4', '5', single digit only. If reply is not 1–5: resend the same question with 'Please reply with a number from 1 to 5. 😢=1, 😕=2, 😐=3, 🙂=4, 😁=5'. Cap retries at 3 per question — after 3 invalid replies, mark session abandoned.
criticalPatterns.ReturnValidators: All new queries and mutations must include returns validator.
criticalPatterns.NoNewTwilioClient: Reuse the exact Twilio REST API call pattern already in whatsapp.ts. Do not introduce a new Twilio SDK dependency.

industryContext.approach: Sequential conversational flow (one question per message, numbered text replies) is the production-proven standard for WhatsApp-based wellness forms via Twilio. Used by sports clubs across Ireland, UK, and Europe where WhatsApp is the primary team communication tool.
industryContext.completionRates: Industry data: push notification → in-app form ~30%, SMS link → web form ~25%, WhatsApp conversational ~72%, WhatsApp Flows (Meta direct API) ~83%. The sequential conversational approach is the best achievable within Twilio's constraints.
industryContext.alternativeConsidered: WhatsApp Flows (Meta Business Cloud API directly) would provide a higher completion rate (~83%) and a more native app-like form experience — a single message opens a multi-page form, the player selects values on-screen (no text replies needed), and submits all at once. However, this requires migrating from Twilio to Meta's Cloud API directly, a Meta Business Manager verification process, and Flows template approval. Recommended as a Phase 8b upgrade path once the conversational flow is proven.
industryContext.competitorLandscape: TrainingPeaks, Catapult, Hudl Teamwork use in-app push → web form (30–40% completion). No major sports platform currently offers native WhatsApp wellness check-ins as a primary channel. This is a genuine competitive differentiator for Irish/European club sports where WhatsApp is dominant.

userStories.title: Backend: whatsappWellnessSessions Schema + Player Phone Registration
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the session state table and player phone registration schema so that the WhatsApp wellness flow has a type-safe foundation that integrates with existing WhatsApp infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add whatsappWellnessSessions table to packages/backend/convex/schema.ts:

playerIdentityId (v.id('playerIdentities')), organizationId (string), sessionDate (string YYYY-MM-DD), status (v.union of 'pending'|'in_progress'|'completed'|'abandoned' literals), enabledDimensions (v.array(v.string()) — snapshot of enabled dimensions at send time), currentDimensionIndex (number, starts at 0), collectedResponses (v.any() — object mapping dimensionName to number), phoneNumber (string E.164), sentAt (number), optional startedAt (number), optional completedAt (number), expiresAt (number — sentAt + 8 hours), optional dailyHealthCheckId (v.id('dailyPlayerHealthChecks') — set after completion), invalidReplyCount (number, default 0). Indexes: by_phone_and_date [phoneNumber, sessionDate], by_player_and_date [playerIdentityId, sessionDate], by_status_and_date [status, sessionDate]

Add WhatsApp opt-in fields to playerWellnessSettings table (Phase 4 table — check if it exists; if not, add to whatever table stores player contact preferences): whatsappOptIn (v.optional(v.boolean()) default false), whatsappNumber (v.optional(v.string()) E.164 format), whatsappOptedInAt (v.optional(v.number())). Add index by_whatsapp_number [whatsappNumber] to enable phone→player lookup.

If playerWellnessSettings does not have a phone index, add it or create a new separate playerContactPreferences table with: playerIdentityId, organizationId, whatsappNumber (string), whatsappOptIn (boolean), whatsappOptedInAt (number). Index: by_whatsapp_number [whatsappNumber], by_player [playerIdentityId].
Add new queries/mutations to packages/backend/convex/models/playerHealthChecks.ts (or a new packages/backend/convex/models/whatsappWellness.ts):

getActiveWellnessSession(phoneNumber): looks up active whatsappWellnessSessions for today using by_phone_and_date index where status is 'pending' or 'in_progress' and expiresAt > now. Returns session or null.
getPlayerByWhatsappNumber(phoneNumber): looks up playerWellnessSettings (or playerContactPreferences) by whatsappNumber index. Returns playerIdentityId or null.
createWellnessSession(playerIdentityId, organizationId, sessionDate, enabledDimensions, phoneNumber): inserts new whatsappWellnessSessions record with status 'pending', expiresAt = sentAt + 8h.

recordWellnessAnswer(sessionId, dimensionName, answer, isFirstAnswer): patches collectedResponses with new answer. Increments currentDimensionIndex. Sets startedAt if isFirstAnswer. Updates status to 'in_progress'.
completeWellnessSession(sessionId, dailyHealthCheckId): sets status to 'completed', completedAt = now, dailyHealthCheckId.
abandonWellnessSession(sessionId, reason): sets status to 'abandoned'.

registerPlayerWhatsapp(playerIdentityId, organizationId, phoneNumber): upserts whatsappOptIn fields. Validates E.164 format (must start with +, digits only after). Rejects duplicate phone numbers already registered to a different player in the same org.
deregisterPlayerWhatsapp(playerIdentityId): sets whatsappOptIn to false, clears whatsappNumber.
Run npx -w packages/backend convex codegen — all types must pass.
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: Read the existing schema.ts carefully before deciding whether to add WhatsApp fields to playerWellnessSettings or create a new table. The key constraint is the by_whatsapp_number index for the phone→player lookup in the webhook handler. US-P8-001 must be complete before all other Phase 8 stories.

userStories.title: Player WhatsApp Opt-In & Phone Registration (Player Settings)
userStories.description: As an adult player, I want to register my WhatsApp number and opt in to receiving daily wellness check-ins via WhatsApp so that I can complete my check-in without opening the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'WhatsApp Wellness Check-Ins' section to the player settings page (/orgs/[orgId]/player/settings) — below the Wellness Dimensions and Wellness Access sections from Phase 4
Section shows: toggle 'Receive daily wellness check via WhatsApp', phone number input (E.164 format with country code selector or prefix), and 'Verify Number' button
Phone number input: show '+' prefix with country code dropdown defaulting to player's country. Format: +353 87 123 4567 style. On save: normalise to E.164 before storing.

VERIFICATION STEP: On clicking 'Verify Number', call a sendWhatsappVerification action that sends a 6-digit PIN to the entered number via Twilio ('Your PlayerARC WhatsApp wellness verification code is: 123456. Reply with this code to confirm.'). Player enters the PIN in the settings UI. On correct PIN: registerPlayerWhatsapp mutation called. This prevents players entering someone else's number.
Opt-in toggle: only visible and enabled AFTER phone number is verified. Toggle ON = registerPlayerWhatsapp sets whatsappOptIn: true. Toggle OFF = deregisterPlayerWhatsapp.

WhatsApp Business Policy disclosure shown below the toggle: 'By enabling this, you consent to receive wellness check messages from PlayerARC via WhatsApp. Message and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to unsubscribe at any time.'
Verified number shown masked: '+353 87 *** 4567'. 'Change number' link lets player re-register (sends new verification PIN, clears old opt-in until re-verified).

If player opts out via WhatsApp STOP command (Twilio handles this externally): on next load of settings page, check whatsappOptIn — if Twilio has blocked the number, sync state and show 'You have opted out via WhatsApp. Re-enable below to resume.' (requires Twilio opt-out status check via API call on page load or via webhook).
Note shown: 'You will only receive a WhatsApp message on days when you haven't already checked in via the app.'
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: US-P8-001 must be complete first. The verification PIN flow requires a short-lived verification code — store it in a small table or use a scheduled mutation with a 10-minute expiry. Find how existing verification flows work in the codebase before implementing a new one.

userStories.title: Admin WhatsApp Wellness Scheduling Configuration
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to configure the daily WhatsApp wellness dispatch — which teams receive it, at what time, and on which days — so that messages arrive at the right moment for my players.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend the existing 'Wellness Reminders' admin settings section (Phase 4, US-P4-009) with a new 'WhatsApp' subsection
Master toggle: 'Enable WhatsApp wellness check-ins' (org-wide). Only shown if the org has Twilio WhatsApp configured (TWILIO_WHATSAPP_FROM env var is set).
When enabled, show configuration fields:
Send time: time picker (HH:MM in 24h, 15-minute increments). Default: 08:00.
Timezone: dropdown of IANA timezone zones with org's timezone pre-selected. Default: Europe/Dublin.

Active days: multi-select checkboxes for Mon/Tue/Wed/Thu/Fri/Sat/Sun. Default: all 7 days. Also offer preset buttons: 'Every Day', 'Weekdays Only', 'Training Days' (requires team schedule integration — if not available, show 'Every Day' and 'Weekdays Only' only).
Target teams: multi-select dropdown of org teams. Default: all teams (empty array = all). Admin can restrict to specific teams.
Skip players who've already checked in via app: toggle (always ON, cannot be disabled — this is a system guarantee).
Save button: calls upsertOrgWhatsappWellnessConfig mutation (add to wellness config in schema or admin settings — find existing org config table).
Preview panel: 'Next dispatch: [weekday] [date] at [time] [timezone] to [N] opted-in players across [M] teams.'
Warning if no players are opted in: 'No players have registered a WhatsApp number yet. Players can opt in from their Settings page.'
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: Read the existing org admin settings structure before adding new config. Find whether there is an existing org-level config table to add the WhatsApp config to, or if it needs its own table. The upsertOrgWhatsappWellnessConfig mutation must be in the appropriate models file.

userStories.title: Daily WhatsApp Wellness Dispatch (Scheduled Cron)
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want the system to automatically send wellness check messages to opted-in players at the configured time each day so that no manual action is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new Convex scheduled function (cron) that runs every 15 minutes: packages/backend/convex/jobs/whatsappWellnessDispatch.ts (or extend an existing jobs file).

Cron logic: query all orgs with WhatsApp wellness enabled. For each org: check if current UTC time (converted to org timezone) matches the configured send time (within a 15-minute window). If yes: trigger dispatch for that org.
Per-org dispatch logic (run as a Convex action):
1. Find all opted-in players (whatsappOptIn: true, whatsappNumber set) in the target teams for this org.

2. For each opted-in player: check if they already have a dailyPlayerHealthChecks record for today using by_player_and_date index. If yes: SKIP (do not send — optionally send a 'already checked in' message if it was their first time skipping).
3. For each player to send to: fetch their whatsappWellnessSessions for today — if a session already exists (sent but not completed), skip duplicate send.
4. Fetch the player's getWellnessSettings to get their enabledDimensions list.
5. Create a whatsappWellnessSessions record (status: 'pending').
6. Send the initial WhatsApp message via Twilio (see message format below).
OUTBOUND MESSAGE FORMAT — Initial message (this IS the first question, combined with greeting):

'Good morning [First Name]! 🏃

[Club Name] daily wellness check.

Question 1 of [N]: [Dimension Label]
[Dimension emoji + description]

Reply with:
1️⃣ Very Poor
2️⃣ Poor
3️⃣ Neutral
4️⃣ Good
5️⃣ Great'

Dimension labels: Sleep Quality = 'How did you sleep last night?', Energy = 'How is your energy level today?', Food Intake = 'How was your food intake today?', Water Intake = 'How well hydrated were you today?', Mood = 'How is your mood?', Motivation = 'How motivated are you for training?', Physical Feeling = 'How does your body feel physically?', Muscle Recovery = 'How are your muscles today?'

TEMPLATE REQUIREMENT: The initial message must be sent as a Twilio Content Template in production (required by WhatsApp Business Policy for first messages or messages sent >24h after the player's last inbound message). Create and document the template content in the codebase. In sandbox mode this requirement is waived.
Log the outbound Twilio messageSid against the whatsappWellnessSessions record for threading/debugging.
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: Find existing cron patterns in the codebase (e.g. jobs/graduations.ts) before creating a new cron. The 15-minute polling interval handles different org timezones without needing per-org scheduled jobs. Twilio outbound call: reuse the exact pattern from existing whatsapp.ts — do NOT create a new Twilio client.

userStories.title: Inbound Reply Processing — Wellness Session Router
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need the processIncomingMessage action to detect and handle wellness session replies so that player answers are correctly captured and the conversation advances.
acceptanceCriteria: READ packages/backend/convex/actions/whatsapp.ts FULLY before modifying. Understand the full processIncomingMessage flow and all existing branches.
Add wellness session check as the FIRST routing step in processIncomingMessage, before any coach processing:
Step 1: normalizePhoneNumber(from) — already done
Step 2: call getActiveWellnessSession(normalizedPhone)
Step 3: if active session found → call handleWellnessReply(session, messageBody) → RETURN. Do NOT continue to coach processing.
Step 4: existing coach processing continues unchanged for all other messages
Implement handleWellnessReply(session, messageBody) as a new function in whatsapp.ts (or extract to packages/backend/convex/lib/whatsappWellnessHandler.ts for separation):
Parse messageBody: trim whitespace, extract first character/token. Valid if it is '1', '2', '3', '4', or '5'.

If invalid: increment invalidReplyCount. If invalidReplyCount < 3: resend same question with: 'Please reply with a number from 1 to 5:\n1️⃣ Very Poor  2️⃣ Poor  3️⃣ Neutral  4️⃣ Good  5️⃣ Great'. If invalidReplyCount >= 3: send 'Too many invalid replies. This wellness session has been closed. Your next check will arrive tomorrow.' then call abandonWellnessSession.
If valid: call recordWellnessAnswer(sessionId, dimensionName, answer, isFirstAnswer).
Check if there is a next dimension (currentDimensionIndex + 1 < enabledDimensions.length):

YES: send next question message: 'Question [N] of [Total]: [Dimension Label]
[Dimension description]

Reply 1–5:
1️⃣ Very Poor  2️⃣ Poor  3️⃣ Neutral  4️⃣ Good  5️⃣ Great'
NO: all dimensions answered → proceed to completion (US-P8-006 completion step)
COMMAND INTERCEPTION: Before answer parsing, check for special commands:
'STOP': handled by Twilio automatically (deregisters entirely). Add logging to mark the wellness opt-out.
'WELLNESSSTOP': call deregisterPlayerWhatsapp. Reply: 'You have opted out of WhatsApp wellness check-ins. You can re-enable this in your PlayerARC settings.'
'SKIP': call abandonWellnessSession. Reply: 'No problem! You can still check in via the PlayerARC app today.'
'HELP': reply with brief wellness help text: 'Reply 1–5 for each question (1=Very Poor, 5=Great). Reply SKIP to skip today. Reply WELLNESSSTOP to opt out of daily messages.'

Add WELLNESS command to the command handler (packages/backend/convex/lib/whatsappCommands.ts): if player sends 'WELLNESS' or 'W' outside of an active session, create a new session and send the first question immediately (on-demand wellness check trigger).
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: Read whatsapp.ts fully before touching it. The session-first routing is critical — without it, player answers will be processed as coach voice notes. Keep the handleWellnessReply logic clean and separate from the coach processing path.

userStories.title: Wellness Session Completion & App Sync
userStories.description: As a player, I want to receive a confirmation message when my WhatsApp wellness check is complete and see the results in my app so that I know my data was saved.
acceptanceCriteria: On completion (all enabledDimensions answered in the session):
1. Build dimensionValues object from session.collectedResponses

2. Call submitDailyHealthCheck mutation (Phase 4) with: playerIdentityId, organizationId, checkDate = session.sessionDate, dimensionValues, enabledDimensions = session.enabledDimensions, submittedOffline: false, source: 'whatsapp' (add source field to dailyPlayerHealthChecks schema if not already present — v.optional(v.union(v.literal('app'), v.literal('whatsapp'))))
3. Call completeWellnessSession(sessionId, dailyHealthCheckId)
4. Compute aggregate score: sum of all dimension values / count of dimensions, round to 1 decimal
5. Send completion WhatsApp message:

'✅ Wellness check complete! 🎉

Your score today: [aggregateScore]/5 [emoji]

[scoreInterpretation]

You can view your full wellness history and trends in the PlayerARC app.

_Reply SKIP anytime to skip a day, or WELLNESSSTOP to opt out._'

6. Score interpretation text: ≤2.0 = 'Take it easy today — listen to your body.', 2.1–3.5 = 'A moderate day — make sure to recover well.', 3.6–4.5 = 'Good energy — have a great session!', 4.6+ = 'Excellent! You're feeling great today! 🔥'

If submitDailyHealthCheck fails (e.g. duplicate record created between session start and completion): call completeWellnessSession without a healthCheckId and send: '✅ Check-in received! (You may have already checked in via the app today — your latest submission is saved.)'

In the player's app (health-check page): WhatsApp-submitted records appear identically to app submissions. If source: 'whatsapp' is set, show a small WhatsApp icon badge next to the date in the history list — 'Checked in via WhatsApp'.
Streak counter (from Phase 4): WhatsApp submissions count toward the streak — query includes both app and WhatsApp sources.
Coach wellness dashboard (Phase 4): WhatsApp-submitted data appears identically to app data — coaches see aggregate score regardless of source.
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: The source field addition to dailyPlayerHealthChecks requires a schema change and codegen run before this story can be implemented. The completion message is the player's payoff — make it clear and positive.

userStories.title: Admin WhatsApp Wellness Delivery Monitoring
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to see which players received and completed WhatsApp wellness checks so that I can track engagement and identify players who haven't opted in.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a 'WhatsApp' tab or section to the admin wellness analytics page (built in Phase 4 US-P4-009)
Today's dispatch summary: 'X messages sent, Y completed (Z%), W skipped (already checked in via app), V opted-in players not yet messaged today'

Player list: table showing each opted-in player with columns: Name, Team, Status (Sent / Completed / Abandoned / Skipped-already-in-app), Completion Time (for completed sessions), Score (aggregate, for completed)

Players without WhatsApp opt-in: separate section 'Players without WhatsApp opt-in ([count])' — list of players who could receive WhatsApp wellness but haven't registered. Admin can send an in-app nudge: 'Register WhatsApp number' notification to selected players.
Historical view: date range picker showing daily completion rates as a bar chart (WhatsApp vs app check-in sources stacked)
Session abandonment reasons: breakdown of abandonment reasons if tracked (expired, skip command, too many invalid replies)
npm run check-types passes.
userStories.notes: Read the existing admin wellness analytics page from Phase 4 before implementing. This is an additive section — do not replace existing analytics.

userStories.title: Phase 8 WhatsApp Wellness E2E Tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests for the WhatsApp wellness flow so that the dispatch, reply parsing, session completion, and app sync all work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test file: apps/web/uat/tests/whatsapp-wellness-phase8.spec.ts (for UI tests) and document manual WhatsApp flow tests separately
UI Test: player settings WhatsApp section shows phone number input and opt-in toggle
UI Test: toggle is disabled until phone number is verified
UI Test: admin WhatsApp config section shows time picker, timezone, target teams
UI Test: admin monitoring table shows opted-in players with status
UI Test: app wellness history shows WhatsApp icon badge on WhatsApp-submitted records
Backend test (via Convex MCP): getActiveWellnessSession returns null when no session exists, returns session when one is active
Backend test: recordWellnessAnswer advances currentDimensionIndex correctly
Backend test: completed session creates dailyPlayerHealthChecks record with correct values
Manual test 1: Register a WhatsApp number in player settings → verify the PIN confirmation flow → confirm whatsappOptIn: true in DB

Manual test 2: Admin enables WhatsApp wellness, sets send time 2 minutes from now → wait → confirm Twilio message received on the registered WhatsApp number → confirm whatsappWellnessSessions record created with status 'pending'
Manual test 3: Reply '3' to first question → confirm next question received → reply to each question → confirm completion message received with aggregate score
Manual test 4: Inspect Convex DB → confirm dailyPlayerHealthChecks record created with correct dimension values and source: 'whatsapp'
Manual test 5: Open app health-check page → confirm WhatsApp-submitted record appears with WhatsApp icon badge
Manual test 6: Submit wellness check via app first (before WhatsApp send time) → confirm WhatsApp message says 'already checked in today' rather than starting a new session
Manual test 7: Reply with invalid input ('abc') → confirm 'please reply 1–5' resent → reply invalid twice more → confirm session abandoned message
Manual test 8: Reply 'SKIP' mid-session → confirm session abandoned, polite skip message received
Manual test 9: Reply 'WELLNESSSTOP' → confirm player deregistered, confirmation message received, DB updated
Manual test 10: Send 'WELLNESS' outside of session time → confirm on-demand session created and first question received
Manual test 11: Wait 8+ hours after session sent → reply to it → confirm 'session expired' message

Manual test 12: Log in as coach on the same WhatsApp number who is also a player with active wellness session → send coach voice note first → confirm wellness session intercepts and receives it as a wellness answer (not voice note). Then after session completes → send another coach voice note → confirm coach processing resumes.
userStories.notes: Manual tests require a real Twilio WhatsApp number (sandbox is fine for testing). US-P8-001 through US-P8-007 must all be complete. The coach+player same-number test (manual test 12) is critical for correctness — must be verified manually.
